data.title: Financials Up as Market Volatility Spurs Banks, Money Managers -- Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions rose as traders bet the sector would benefit from stable interest rates and hot financial markets.

Artificial intelligence stocks and precious metals saw wild swings Thursday -- volatility that's music to the ears of Wall Street traders.

Credit-card processor Mastercard said it plans to cut 4% of its roughly 35,000 employees and book a $200 million restructuring charge, becoming the latest in a series of companies that have announced job cuts in recent days.

President Donald Trump said he would unveil his nominee for the next chair of the Federal Reserve next week.

Fed Chairman Jerome Powell spoke about the importance of central-bank independence during a press conference following the Fed's policy statement.

One brokerage said economic data and the Fed board's confidence in those trends suggest rate cuts may be done for the foreseeable future, quelling fears about central-bank independence.

"We anticipate strong growth and sticky inflation, and regardless of who succeeds Powell, policy will likely be determined primarily by the economic outlook," economists at brokerage BNP Paribas Markets said in a note to clients.

Marsh's fourth-quarter net income rose amid revenue growth across the recently renamed firm's insurance and consulting brands.

First Brands Group founder Patrick James and his brother Edward were indicted by federal prosecutors on charges of defrauding lenders ahead of the auto-parts company's collapse into bankruptcy.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-29-26 1752ET

data.title: Wall Street indexes see red as Big Tech's soaring AI budgets trigger flight
Elon Musk's SpaceX said to consider merger with Tesla, Bloomberg News reports
data.description:
Jan 29 (Reuters) - Elon Musk's SpaceX is considering a potential merger with Tesla, as well as an alternative combination with artificial-intelligence firm xAI, Bloomberg News reported on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Tesla's shares were up 3% after the bell following the report.

(Reporting by Juby Babu in Mexico City; Editing by Pooja Desai)

Consumer Cos Down Amid Housing Market Distress -- Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies fell amid signs of distress from the housing market.

Home Depot has laid off 800 workers associated with its corporate headquarters, part of an effort to boost the company's speed and focus more on its front-line workers.

Home builder PulteGroup posted lower fourth-quarter profit as weak consumer sentiment continued to hurt home sales.

Sherwin-Williams' fourth-quarter net income slipped and the maker of house paint warned that weak demand in the North American Do-it-yourself market was likely to persist this year, even as it forecast earnings growth.

In one good sign for discretionary spending, cruiseline Royal Caribbean reported higher fourth-quarter profit and revenue as customers continued to spend more for onboard services.

Marlboro maker Altria reported lower fourth-quarter profit as cigarette shipment volume declined, dragging down its smokeable product business.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-29-26 1745ET

Arthur J Gallagher's quarterly profit rises on fees, commission growth
data.description:
Jan 29 (Reuters) - Arthur J. Gallagher posted 24.5% rise in fourth-quarter adjusted profit on Thursday, as the insurance broker benefited from stronger commissions and fees amid solid demand.

Insurance spending has proved resilient as businesses and individuals prioritize coverage against financial risks, natural disasters, and other losses, even as they rein in spending elsewhere.

Arthur J. Gallagher's commissions rose to $2.06 billion in the quarter from $1.50 billion in the prior year. Total fees climbed nearly 34.8% to $1.2 billion.

Insurance brokerages act as intermediaries between clients and insurers, guiding customers in selecting policies that best match their needs, instead of selling plans directly. Brokers generate revenue through commissions based on premiums, tying their performance closely to the insurance industry numbers.     

Peer Brown and Brown on Monday reported higher quarterly adjusted profit, helped by rise in commissions and fees. But ?its organic revenue fell 2.8%.

The company reported adjusted net profit of $620 million, or $2.38 per share, for the three months ended December 31, compared with $498 million, or $2.16 per share, in the same period a year earlier.

(Reporting by Pragyan Kalita in Bengaluru; Editing by Leroy Leo)

data.title: Apple acquires Israeli audio AI startup Q.ai
Industrials Up Amid Mixed Earnings, Economic Data -- Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies rose amid mixed earnings and economic data.

Caterpillar rose after the machinery maker said demand from data-center customers for heavy generators spurred growth. Sales across Caterpillar's power and energy unit jumped 23% during the fourth quarter, eclipsing growth in sales of diggers and backhoe loaders.

Dow shares fell after the chemicals giant said it was cutting 4,500 employees as part of a cost-saving program that will lean on artificial intelligence to increase productivity and bolster shareholder returns.

Orders from U.S. factories rose 2.7% in November to $621.6 billion, from $605.4 billion in October.

In his Wednesday press conference, Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell noted that data-center development had boosted overall economic activity.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-29-26 1735ET

data.title: Gold exports from Switzerland up 27% m/m in December as deliveries to UK jump
KLA Posts Higher 2Q Profit On Product and Services Sales Growth
data.description:
By Amira McKee


KLA posted a higher fiscal second quarter profit on gains in products and services revenue.

The semi-conductor equipment provider logged net income of $1.15 billion, or $8.68 per share, in the period ended Dec. 31, compared with $824.5 million, or $6.16 a share, a year earlier.

Adjusted earnings were $8.85 per share. Analysts polled by FactSet were looking for $8.79 per share.

Revenue rose 7.1% to $3.3 billion, beating the $3.25 billion forecast by Wall Street, according to FactSet. Services revenue increased 18%, while products revenue rose 4.2%.

KLA expects third-quarter revenue in the range of $3.2 billion to $3.5 billion and adjusted earnings in the range of $8.30 to $9.86 per share.

Analysts polled by FactSet anticipate third-quarter revenue of $3.29 billion and adjusted earnings of $8.97 per share.

"As we look forward to calendar year 2026, KLA is a key enabler of the AI ecosystem and continues to uniquely benefit from the AI infrastructure buildout across all major growth vectors, including foundry/logic, memory, advanced packaging, and services." CEO Rick Wallace said.


Write to Amira McKee at amira.mckee@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-29-26 1731ET

data.title: China's Montage Technology seeks up to $902 million in Hong Kong listing
data.description:
Jan 30 (Reuters) - China's Montage Technology is looking to raise as much as HK$7.04 billion ($901.95 million) in its Hong Kong listing, according to a filing with the exchange on Friday.

The Shanghai-listed chipmaker is offering 65.9 million H shares at a maximum offer price of HK$106.89 apiece.

($1 = 7.8053 Hong Kong dollars)

(Reporting by Nikita Maria Jino in Bengaluru)

data.title: Gold falls as investors take profits after record high
Materials Up as Gold, Copper Hit Record Highs in Volatile Session -- Materials Roundup
data.description:
Producers of metals and other raw materials rose as metals futures continued a torrid run despite wild intraday swings.

Gold futures rose 0.3% to $5,318.40 a troy ounce, a record high, after a significant selloff early in the session.

Silver also recovered, closing up 0.8% to $114.04 an ounce despite sliding deep into the red early in the session.

Copper for March delivery climbed 4.7% to settle at $6.20 a pound, its highest close based on records dating back to 1972.

"The craziest was gold and silver: they were everywhere," said JJ Kinahan, senior vice president at CBOE Global Markets. "It's almost like gold and silver have become the meme stocks of January 2026."

A wide variety of speculators, from institutional investors to retail traders were chasing gains in metals prices, said Lorenzo Di Mattia, manager of hedge fund Sibilla Global Fund, who is betting against precious metals in a wager that the gains are overdone.

The metals rally has been fueled, in part, by a slide in the U.S. dollar. Part of the dollar selloff is cyclical, Kinahan said.

"The yen has gotten destroyed, so part of it is the yen coming back," Kinahan said. "Also, I think you have a president who [in practically] every public statement is saying the dollar's too strong and hurting our economy."

Packaging company International Paper said it was splitting into two publicly traded companies-keeping its brand for North American operations and creating a new company for Europe, Middle East and Africa operations.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-29-26 1730ET

Mondelez Names CFO Zaramella COO, Initiates Search for New Finance Chief
data.description:
By Paul Ziobro


Mondelez International named Chief Financial Officer Luca Zaramella its chief operating officer and has started a search for a successor for its top finance job.

Zaramella, who has been CFO of the food company since 2018, will hold both positions starting on Feb. 1. In addition to CFO duties, he'll now oversee the company's commercial operations in its four geographical regions as well as corporate sales, marketing and supply chain functions.

The maker of Oreo cookies and Ritz crackers said Thursday it expects to name a new CFO at a later date.

Zaramella joined the company in 1996 in Italy before holding positions of increasing responsibility across the world. Before rising to CFO, he served as corporate controller, treasurer and global head of financial planning and analysis.


Write to Paul Ziobro at paul.ziobro@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-29-26 1726ET

data.title: US Treasury says recent Korean won weakness not aligned to fundamentals
data.description:
SEOUL, Jan 30 (Reuters) - The U.S. Treasury said recent depreciation in the South Korean won was not in line with the Asian country's strong economic fundamentals, in an assessment that was part of a semi-annual currency report.

"Depreciation pressures on the won were acute in the fourth quarter of 2024 as the central bank reduced its policy rate in November and amid the onset of domestic political instability," said the report released on Thursday. "The won depreciated further in late 2025, which was not in line with Korea's strong economic fundamentals."

The rare U.S. assessment on the dollar-won level came after South Korean authorities in December rolled out measures to bolster the currency as it slumped towards the psychologically important level of 1,500 per dollar.

The currency has been under pressure from domestic investors' purchase of overseas stocks and concerns about additional U.S. investment, which was part of a trade deal with President Donald Trump's administration.

The won closed at 1,434.0 per dollar on Thursday, bouncing in recent days after a joint response between Japan and the U.S. helped strengthen the yen.

In its latest semi-annual currency report, the Treasury said no major trading partner met all three criteria for enhanced analysis of currency practices during the last half of 2024 and the first six months of 2025. South Korea remained on a "monitoring list" meriting close attention, but was not accused of currency manipulation.

(Reporting by Cynthia Kim; Editing by Chris Reese and Jamie Freed)

By Cynthia Kim
